text.title: IL LATO SEGRETO DELLA COREA DEL NORD 🇰🇵 (Episodio 2)
text.u: Mamma mia what an absurd day, guys
Everything really happened
Actually it had begun well. Good morning North Korea!
Very good morning, it's 6.30 AM
Let's see the view from the
window, today there is some sun
WOW
it's absurd, I didn't expect it like that, I think this trip
will be full of surprises
Now we have breakfast and then we leave for the first tour in North Korea
it's so cold, guys
There is no one in this hotel
I also got the toast
This is a breakfast of champions, coffee
Russian salad with eggs and we also have eggs and a roll of fried dough
that I don't know how ... I think they should be combined, now I meet the touristic guides in the hotel lobby
when we left we realized that the road was a bit dangerous because it snowed a lot last night
so this morning all the roads were frozen, as soon as we left the city, I began to see
many workers on the street, trying to scrape the ice with very rudimentary tools
the further we got away from the city, the more I saw hundreds
and hundreds and hundreds of workers in the middle of the road, but it was my first day in North Korea
I said ok, it will be so here, they don't have salt, what ... what should I think?
and then we continued to talk, I continued to make friends with the guides,
we were about to tune in, which was incredible with two guards who should supervise me
24 hours on 24, whereas
it seemed we had a pleasant conversation
At a certain point, I started playing the fool as usual and I was trying to make a joke and
in the car, of course there was the driver who is driving
in front of me there was one of the guides
and next to me there was another guide
and the guide in front of me was looking at me because I was telling the story, the one next was doing the same
she was looking at me, waiting for me to finish the joke while I was talking
I looked up at the street, and I saw a boy
crouching on the roadside who was working, he was doing something, suddenly he got up
and he crossed the street abruptly
and then the driver began to honk to let him know he had to move because we were coming,
he got scared
he tried to go back in terror but he slipped, and he slipped in front of the car
The driver tried to brake but the road was frozen and therefore the car
slipped and went
over him, it went completely over him and the thud of the body hitting the car
is something chilling
When I heard it
I said "god, he killed him"
in front of me I saw the boy who froze, looked to
the car
and he could no longer do anything because the car was already over him
and therefore without knowing if we had passed over it if we had hit it sideways
the car continued to slip
it continued to slide towards the guard rail and towards a ravine where there is also a frozen lake
so we were all terrified
time has frozen for us too, I instinctively did so
blocking the guide next to me because it is something my father always does
so now I do it instinctively trying to make a kind of safety belt
a kind of safety belt for the guide
Fortunately the car stopped a little so from the guard rail
when we stopped, when I realized that we were safe I stopped for a moment
I thought
if I had the courage
to turn around to see how the boy was, clearly I took my breath I turned around
and from the glass of the car behind I saw a dead body, a lifeless body
shaken by 5-6 people who had piled up on him trying to awaken him
my blood froze, I went out and
slowly because every single step
I had to make an effort to go on and see how the boy was
the closer I got, the more I felt the rattle of this this boy, he would have been 14-15 years old, he was very young
what he was doing there I don't know
The boy's rattle that increases more and more when I approached him, when I was just above him, in front of him
I saw
he had gritted his teeth very hard and so much blood
was coming out of his mouth
one of the guys,
one of the people who were there, one of the workers took off... in front of me, all this in front of me, he took off his buckle
and began to pry on his teeth trying to open his mouth
Because his tongue had probably gone wrong so he could no longer breathe
I turned to the guides and they were more terrified than me, they didn't know what to do, the driver was in shock
so I tried to talk them I tried to make them understand that I didn't give a damn about the tour
the boy must take our car, he had to go
to the nearest hospital, the problem was that we were two hours from Pyongyang and we were in the middle of nowhere
We were on this road which is completely frozen
and a guy who was working here with the others slipped just while we arrived and the driver completely overwhelmed him
I don't know if
If he's died or not
Now our driver is taking him to the hospital but we got stuck here
in the middle of nowhere
there are no cars, no one touch down here, there are only the workers who are clearing the road
only the workers who are clearing the road
I think the boy died, I think he died
so the driver moved the boy to the car
who
who was really in critical condition
I can't
even explain it but
I really didn't know where and how to look at him, he was really half dead. A couple of minutes later I saw that actually
the other workers went back to work as if nothing had happened and it seemed to me as if
for them it was clearly a drama but the most important thing was to clean the road because for
the country it was the most important thing, the other things were simply a route accident. I believe
a boy's relative
passed out and they left him there
lying on the snow
If a small bolt falls. the gears of the regime must continue to turn without stopping
continue to turn without stopping. But if you visit North Korea, those gears are perfectly hidden
until you doubt everything horrible you read and heard about this country. Because of
an unpredictable mistake I am now inside the gears next to some people
transformed into machines with the absurd task of scraping snow from a highway hundreds of kilometers long with shovels
the next day they would do exactly the same job, and the next one again
and again, and again, probably for all the winters of their lives
it seemed that I was more shocked than they were,
they were continuing without stopping
with an incredible dedication to cleaning the street, while instead that boy was dying
We stayed there for at least two hours
we are stuck here, we are stuck here
on a frozen road in North Korea, I never imagined it
Now we are walking towards
the mountain where we had to go but it is far away
at least two hours by car so I don't know how we will get there. They told me let's walk, go to the mountain
it's so far
now I think she is explaining everything happened
I believe we have found a lift
On the way back the atmosphere was very different in the car nobody spoke
the guides were as shaken as I was but I did not understand if they were more concerned about the accident or the reputation of the travel agency
or about the reputation of the travel agency
In the following days I asked several times about the boy
but the answer has always been: "he's better, better."
I never knew the truth
Today was simply surreal
I felt helpless, I didn't know how to help that boy, it's better going to bed today ... hopefully tomorrow will be better
